_comment: Sets of Microsoft Graph Application permissions that, held together by a single application/service principal, enable privilege escalation even if each is not individually maximal. A combination matches when ALL of its permissions are present in the app's granted permissions. Curated heuristic based on documented Entra ID escalation paths (SpecterOps and others).

dangerous_combinations.name: Self-service God Mode (manage apps + grant app roles)
dangerous_combinations.rationale: The app can create/modify any application AND grant app role assignments to any principal - so it can grant itself (or a new app it controls) arbitrary Microsoft Graph permissions, escalating to effectively unlimited directory control.

dangerous_combinations.name: Manage apps + manage directory roles
dangerous_combinations.rationale: The app can both control application objects (add credentials, act as any app) and assign Entra directory roles (e.g. Global Administrator), giving multiple independent paths to full tenant takeover.

dangerous_combinations.name: Grant app roles + manage directory roles
dangerous_combinations.rationale: The app can grant arbitrary app role assignments and assign privileged directory roles, allowing it to escalate any principal (including itself) to full control.

dangerous_combinations.name: Full directory write + grant app roles
dangerous_combinations.rationale: Broad directory write combined with the ability to grant app role assignments enables creating/controlling principals and granting them powerful permissions.
